interviewStages: Behavioral: customer focus, working with PM/design, design-driven culture
codingTopics: OO design: model a business domain such as workflows with triggers and actions
Multi-tenant data structures: per-tenant rate limiting, isolation
Streaming: rolling aggregates over tenant events
Graph and DAG: workflow evaluation, dependency resolution
Search and filters: AND/OR queries over records
systemDesignTopics: Design a flexible board system supporting any column type and any view (Kanban, calendar, Gantt)
Design real-time collaboration on shared boards with thousands of users
Design automation engine: when X happens, do Y

coding: At Monday.com, model a workflow engine with triggers, conditions, and actions. Evaluate a workflow DAG and detect cycles.
Implement a per-tenant rate limiter so one customer cannot exhaust shared capacity. Discuss the token-bucket design.
Given a stream of tenant events, maintain rolling per-entity scores with bounded memory.
Implement a filter query over records supporting AND/OR on custom fields. Discuss indexing for a multi-tenant store.
Deduplicate records via fuzzy clustering on name and email. Discuss the matching and complexity.
behavioral: Tell me about a project you are proud of at Monday.com scale and the hardest tradeoff you made.
Give me an example that shows customer focus, working with pm/design, design-driven culture.
Describe a time you disagreed with a teammate or manager. How did it resolve?
Tell me about a time you took ownership of an ambiguous problem end to end.
systemDesign: Design a flexible board system supporting any column type and any view (Kanban, calendar, Gantt).
Design real-time collaboration on shared boards with thousands of users.
Design automation engine: when X happens, do Y.
values: Monday.com succeeds when its customers do. Describe a time you solved for the customer even at a cost to the team.
Tell me about a time you handled a tricky multi-tenant isolation or permissions problem.
How do you balance enterprise customization against product simplicity?
other: How does Monday.com compare to Asana or Smartsheet?

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Monday.com
monday.com ↗

Monday.com is the design-forward work management platform — used by 200K+ companies for project management, CRM, marketing, and HR workflows. Israeli-headquartered, public since 2021. The interview emphasizes complex data modeling, customizable workflow engines, and frontend polish.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding phone (DSA medium) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 3–4 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a flexible board system supporting any column type and any view (Kanban, calendar, Gantt)

- Design real-time collaboration on shared boards with thousands of users

- Design automation engine: when X happens, do Y

- Coding: medium DSA, often with graph or expression-tree framing

- Behavioral: customer focus, working with PM/design, design-driven culture

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $140K–$175K total (US); ₪500K–₪650K (Israel)

- Senior SE: $200K–$270K (US)

- Staff: $290K–$390K (US)

- Principal: $400K–$540K (US)

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in TypeScript/React (frontend) and Ruby (backend)

- Understand real-time collaboration patterns and conflict resolution

- Brush up on flexible-schema data modeling (EAV, document-oriented)

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Monday.com remote-friendly?

Hybrid in Tel Aviv (HQ), NYC, London, Sydney. Some engineering roles remote within supported countries.

How does Monday.com compare to Asana or Smartsheet?

Monday wins on design and visual flexibility. Asana on technical-team adoption. Smartsheet on enterprise spreadsheet-style. Comp is comparable across the three.

What is the engineering culture?

Israeli tech culture: direct, fast-paced, pragmatic. Strong design culture. Engineers ship visible product changes frequently.
